frames: The agent stands upright with both feet on the ground, arms bent at the elbows and held in front of its chest.
It begins to lean forward, lifting its right foot slightly off the ground while its arms remain bent.
The agent leans further, bending its left knee and lifting its right leg higher, with arms still bent.
Its torso is significantly bent forward, and the right leg is extended backward, while the left leg supports its weight.
The agent starts to straighten its torso, swinging its right leg forward as it prepares to place it down.
Both feet are now on the ground, and the agent is mostly upright, with arms still bent at the chest.
It lifts its left leg, bending it at the knee, while leaning slightly to the right, maintaining balance on its right foot.
The left leg extends forward and downward, with the torso leaning forward, indicating a continuation of a walking motion.
